data.title: Uber partners with Flytrex for Uber Eats drone delivery
data.description:
As part of the company’s efforts to build a multimodal delivery service, Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER)  announced a partnership with Flytrex to launch drone deliveries by the end of this year.

Flytrex is one of only four drone delivery providers authorized by the Federal Aviation Administration for Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) operations, a distinction that will enable Uber (NYSE:UBER) to make deliveries in minutes while avoiding traffic and reducing emissions.

The collaboration will combine Flytrex’s autonomous drone delivery service with the Uber Eats platform, creating a fully integrated end-to-end experience “designed for speed, safety, and scale.”

As part of the partnership, Uber (NYSE:UBER) will make an investment in Flytrex to accelerate the development and deployment of drone delivery technology.

“Autonomous drones are the future of food delivery—fast, affordable, and hands-free. Flytrex has already delivered over 200,000 meals to suburban households in the past three years,” Flytrex’s Executive Chairman Noam Bardin said in a statement.

data.title: The slump in freight demand in the U.S. continued in August
data.description:
The latest Cass Freight Index update for August indicated sustained freight weakness, with total shipment and expenditure levels remaining under pressure, despite some tentative positive data in truckload and container volumes.

During August, shipments dropped 1.5% on a month-over-month basis and 9.3% year-over-year, extending a trend of falling volume seen throughout 2023 and 2024. The year-over-year decline for the month was the largest since October 2023.

Freight expenditures, which represent total spending on freight, were down 2.8% from July and 0.4% below August 2024 levels. Truckload freight showed slight improvement compared to last year, but less-than-truckload volume fell sharply, partially offsetting growth in container and intermodal categories.

The long freight slump was attributed by Cass Information Systems to persistent headwinds, including trade uncertainty and shifts in freight market segments. It was noted that the freight market is trending toward another significant annual decline, adding to the cumulative drops in prior years.

Publicly traded stocks that could be impacted by the demand trends include major logistics and trucking companies such as J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL), Werner Enterprises (WERN), Heartland Express (HTLD), XPO (XPO), and Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX), as well as rail operators like Union Pacific (UNP) and CSX Corporation (CSX). The report also has significance for FedEx (FDX) and United Parcel Service (UPS) on a broad level.

data.title: DuPont lays out post-spin strategy, sets 2028 growth targets at Investor Day
data.description:
DuPont (NYSE:DD) on Thursday said it will outline its strategy as a streamlined company ahead of the planned November 1 spin-off of its electronics unit, Qnity Electronics, setting medium-term growth targets and recasting its 2025 guidance to reflect discontinued operations.

At its 2025 Investor Day, CEO Lori Koch said the “new DuPont” will focus on high-growth markets, innovation, and operational excellence to accelerate value creation.

“We are well positioned to drive profitable growth,” Koch said in a statement, adding that the company’s balance sheet strength gives it flexibility to both invest and return cash to shareholders.

The company is targeting 3% to 4% organic sales growth, 150 to 200 basis points of ebitda margin expansion and 8% to 10% adjusted earnings per share growth annually through 2028, alongside more than 90% free cash flow conversion. Management also emphasized disciplined capital allocation and plans to codify operational and commercial best practices into a unified business system.

DuPont (NYSE:DD) also updated its financial outlook. For the third quarter of 2025, it now expects sales of about $2.98 billion, operating ebitda of $805 million, and adjusted EPS of $1.06, reflecting the reclassification of its Aramids business as discontinued operations. Full-year 2025 guidance, recast to account for both the Aramids divestiture and the upcoming Qnity spin, calls for $6.87 billion in sales and $1.58 billion in operating ebitda. EPS guidance will be updated on the company’s third-quarter earnings call.

The Aramids divestiture, announced in August, and the Qnity separation are considered major steps in DuPont’s (DD) portfolio transformation. With those moves, DuPont (DD) aims to sharpen its focus on businesses aligned with secular growth drivers such as advanced materials, industrial applications, healthcare and sustainable solutions.

data.title: Allstate estimates $213M of catastrophe losses in August
data.description:
Allstate (NYSE:ALL) estimated its August catastrophe losses  were $218M, or $168M after tax, up from $184M, or $145M after tax, in July, the company said on Thursday.

August 2025's catastrophe losses included 10 events, with ~70%  of the losses tied to three wind and hail events. Total catastrophe losses for July and August came to $397M, or $313M after tax.

Allstate's (NYSE:ALL) protection policies in force increased 0.3% M/M and 0.9% Y/Y to 38.01M as of Aug. 31, 2025. Homeowners' policies in force increased 2.1% Y/Y to 7.62M, while commercial lines PIF dropped 29% Y/Y to 173K. Auto PIF gained 1.0% Y/Y to 25.3M.

data.title: Beam Global stock soars on U.S. patent for smart battery solutions
data.description:
• Beam Global (NASDAQ:BEEM) was granted another new patent by the United States Patent Office, it said on Thursday, sending shares up over 22% in premarket trading.
  • The patent covers Beam Global’s Phase Change Composite (PCC™) material for lithium-ion batteries and is titled Smart Phase Change Composite for Passive Thermal Management.
  • Beam (NASDAQ:BEEM) said its innovation makes batteries significantly more robust for extreme environments, like freezing winters in Serbia and hot deserts in the Middle East, where the company has recently expanded.
